comment_text.sn35.135:1.3: According to MN 50:22.2, this is a name for the Great Hell. It is, therefore, a literal name for hell, not a metaphor for hell as nothing more than sense experience. The hell called “The Mighty Fever” is described in similar terms at SN 56.43.

root_text.sn35.135:1.3: Diṭṭhā mayā, bhikkhave, chaphassāyatanikā nāma nirayā.
root_text.sn35.135:1.4: Tattha yaṁ kiñci cakkhunā rūpaṁ passati aniṭṭharūpaṁyeva passati, no iṭṭharūpaṁ; akantarūpaṁyeva passati, no kantarūpaṁ; amanāparūpaṁyeva passati, no manāparūpaṁ.
root_text.sn35.135:1.9: yaṁ kiñci manasā dhammaṁ vijānāti aniṭṭharūpaṁyeva vijānāti, no iṭṭharūpaṁ; akantarūpaṁyeva vijānāti, no kantarūpaṁ; amanāparūpaṁyeva vijānāti, no manāparūpaṁ.
root_text.sn35.135:1.10: Lābhā vo, bhikkhave, suladdhaṁ vo, bhikkhave,
root_text.sn35.135:1.12: Diṭṭhā mayā, bhikkhave, chaphassāyatanikā nāma saggā.
root_text.sn35.135:1.13: Tattha yaṁ kiñci cakkhunā rūpaṁ passati iṭṭharūpaṁyeva passati, no aniṭṭharūpaṁ; kantarūpaṁyeva passati, no akantarūpaṁ; manāparūpaṁyeva passati, no amanāparūpaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn35.135:1.15: yaṁ kiñci manasā dhammaṁ vijānāti iṭṭharūpaṁyeva vijānāti, no aniṭṭharūpaṁ; kantarūpaṁyeva vijānāti, no akantarūpaṁ; manāparūpaṁyeva vijānāti, no amanāparūpaṁ.
root_text.sn35.135:1.16: Lābhā vo, bhikkhave, suladdhaṁ vo, bhikkhave,

translation_text.sn35.135:1.2: to have the opportunity to lead the spiritual life.
translation_text.sn35.135:1.3: I’ve seen the hell named ‘Related to the Six Fields of Contact’.
translation_text.sn35.135:1.4: There, whatever sight you see with your eye is unlikable, not likable; undesirable, not desirable; unpleasant, not pleasant.
translation_text.sn35.135:1.9: Whatever idea you know with your mind is unlikable, not likable; undesirable, not desirable; unpleasant, not pleasant.
translation_text.sn35.135:1.10: You’re fortunate, mendicants, so very fortunate,
translation_text.sn35.135:1.11: to have the opportunity to lead the spiritual life.
translation_text.sn35.135:1.12: I’ve seen the heaven called ‘Related to the Six Fields of Contact’.
translation_text.sn35.135:1.13: There, whatever sight you see with your eye is likable, not unlikable; desirable, not undesirable; pleasant, not unpleasant.
translation_text.sn35.135:1.15: Whatever idea you know with your mind is likable, not unlikable; desirable, not undesirable; pleasant, not unpleasant.
translation_text.sn35.135:1.16: You’re fortunate, mendicants, so very fortunate,
translation_text.sn35.135:1.17: to have the opportunity to lead the spiritual life.”
